comment_text.sn12.20:1.2: Dependent origination is the process, dependently originated phenomena are the products.
comment_text.sn12.20:2.3: The principles of the Dhamma are not impermanent, since they are not things that exist. Rather, they are laws of nature that describe a process or a pattern which is always observable, so it is always true to describe it in that way. At AN 3.136 the same idea applies to impermanence, suffering and not-self. Also see Poṭṭhapāda at DN 9:31.10. | For “surety” (niyāmatā) compare Rig Veda 4.3.9, “With truth I invoke the truth set down” (ṛtena ṛtaṁ niyatam). It is related to the idea that the stream-enterer “steps into the sure path”, i.e. enters the noble eightfold path, and is therefore “sure” in the sense of confident, and “sure” in the sense of being bound for awakening.
comment_text.sn12.20:3.17: This phrase is also at SN 12.1:2.14. Note that there is no corresponding “dependent cessation”. The ending of dependent origination is Nibbāna, extinguishment.
comment_text.sn12.20:5.2: These are called “irrational thoughts” at MN 2:7.3. These are ways of thinking driven by anxiety over the perpetuation or dissolution of the assumed Self.

root_text.sn12.20:1.2: “Paṭiccasamuppādañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi paṭiccasamuppanne ca dhamme.
root_text.sn12.20:1.3: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn12.20:1.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn12.20:2.3: Uppādā vā tathāgatānaṁ anuppādā vā tathāgatānaṁ, ṭhitāva sā dhātu dhammaṭṭhitatā dhammaniyāmatā idappaccayatā.
root_text.sn12.20:2.5: Abhisambujjhitvā abhisametvā ācikkhati deseti paññāpeti paṭṭhapeti vivarati vibhajati uttānīkaroti.
root_text.sn12.20:3.11: uppādā vā tathāgatānaṁ anuppādā vā tathāgatānaṁ, ṭhitāva sā dhātu dhammaṭṭhitatā dhammaniyāmatā idappaccayatā.
root_text.sn12.20:3.13: Abhisambujjhitvā abhisametvā ācikkhati deseti paññāpeti paṭṭhapeti vivarati vibhajati uttānīkaroti.
root_text.sn12.20:3.16: Iti kho, bhikkhave, yā tatra tathatā avitathatā anaññathatā idappaccayatā—
root_text.sn12.20:4.2: Jarāmaraṇaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ saṅkhataṁ paṭiccasamuppannaṁ khayadhammaṁ vayadhammaṁ virāgadhammaṁ nirodhadhammaṁ.
root_text.sn12.20:4.3: Jāti, bhikkhave, aniccā saṅkhatā paṭiccasamuppannā khayadhammā vayadhammā virāgadhammā nirodhadhammā.
root_text.sn12.20:4.4: Bhavo, bhikkhave, anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno khayadhammo vayadhammo virāgadhammo nirodhadhammo.
root_text.sn12.20:4.13: avijjā, bhikkhave, aniccā saṅkhatā paṭiccasamuppannā khayadhammā vayadhammā virāgadhammā nirodhadhammā.
root_text.sn12.20:5.1: Yato kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa ‘ayañca paṭiccasamuppādo, ime ca paṭiccasamuppannā dhammā’ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti, so vata pubbantaṁ vā paṭidhāvissati:
root_text.sn12.20:5.2: ‘ahosiṁ nu kho ahaṁ atītamaddhānaṁ, nanu kho ahosiṁ atītamaddhānaṁ, kiṁ nu kho ahosiṁ atītamaddhānaṁ, kathaṁ nu kho ahosiṁ atītamaddhānaṁ, kiṁ hutvā kiṁ ahosiṁ nu kho ahaṁ atītamaddhānan’ti;
root_text.sn12.20:5.4: ‘bhavissāmi nu kho ahaṁ anāgatamaddhānaṁ, nanu kho bhavissāmi anāgatamaddhānaṁ, kiṁ nu kho bhavissāmi anāgatamaddhānaṁ, kathaṁ nu kho bhavissāmi anāgatamaddhānaṁ, kiṁ hutvā kiṁ bhavissāmi nu kho ahaṁ anāgatamaddhānan’ti;
root_text.sn12.20:5.5: etarahi vā paccuppannaṁ addhānaṁ ajjhattaṁ kathaṅkathī bhavissati:
root_text.sn12.20:5.6: ‘ahaṁ nu khosmi, no nu khosmi, kiṁ nu khosmi, kathaṁ nu khosmi, ayaṁ nu kho satto kuto āgato, so kuhiṁ gamissatī’ti—
root_text.sn12.20:5.9: Tathā hi, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa ayañca paṭiccasamuppādo ime ca paṭiccasamuppannā dhammā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā”ti.

translation_text.sn12.20:1.2: “Mendicants, I will teach you dependent origination and dependently originated phenomena.
translation_text.sn12.20:1.3: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn12.20:2.2: Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death.
translation_text.sn12.20:2.3: Whether Realized Ones arise or not, this law of nature persists, this regularity of natural principles, this surety of natural principles, specific conditionality.
translation_text.sn12.20:2.4: A Realized One awakens to this and comprehends it,
translation_text.sn12.20:2.5: then he explains, teaches, asserts, establishes, clarifies, analyzes, and reveals it.
translation_text.sn12.20:2.7: ‘Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death.’
translation_text.sn12.20:3.1: Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.2: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.3: Craving is a requirement for grasping …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.4: Feeling is a requirement for craving …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.5: Contact is a requirement for feeling …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.6: The six sense fields are requirements for contact …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.7: Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.8: Consciousness is a requirement for name and form …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.9: Choices are a requirement for consciousness …
translation_text.sn12.20:3.10: Ignorance is a requirement for choices.
translation_text.sn12.20:3.11: Whether Realized Ones arise or not, this law of nature persists, this regularity of natural principles, this surety of natural principles, specific conditionality.
translation_text.sn12.20:3.12: A Realized One awakens to this and comprehends it,
translation_text.sn12.20:3.13: then he explains, teaches, asserts, establishes, clarifies, analyzes, and reveals it.
translation_text.sn12.20:3.16: So the fact that this is real, not unreal, not otherwise; the specific conditionality of it:
translation_text.sn12.20:4.1: And what are the dependently originated phenomena?
translation_text.sn12.20:4.2: Old age and death are impermanent, conditioned, dependently originated, liable to end, vanish, fade away, and cease.
translation_text.sn12.20:4.13: Ignorance is impermanent, conditioned, dependently originated, liable to end, vanish, fade away, and cease.
translation_text.sn12.20:4.14: These are called the dependently originated phenomena.
translation_text.sn12.20:5.1: When a noble disciple has clearly seen with right wisdom this dependent origination and these dependently originated phenomena as they are, it will be quite impossible for them to turn back to the first beginning, thinking:
translation_text.sn12.20:5.2: ‘Did I exist in the past? Did I not exist in the past? What was I in the past? How was I in the past? After being what, what did I become in the past?’
translation_text.sn12.20:5.3: Or to turn forward to the final end, thinking:
translation_text.sn12.20:5.4: ‘Will I exist in the future? Will I not exist in the future? What will I be in the future? How will I be in the future? After being what, what will I become in the future?’
translation_text.sn12.20:5.5: Or to be undecided about the present, thinking:
translation_text.sn12.20:5.6: ‘Am I? Am I not? What am I? How am I? This sentient being—where did it come from? And where will it go?’
translation_text.sn12.20:5.9: Because that noble disciple has clearly seen with right wisdom this dependent origination and these dependently originated phenomena as they are.”

variant_text.sn12.20:5.2: ahosiṁ nu kho ahaṁ → nu khvāhaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
